target.title: fix(slack): inherit thread context for media sends on wake turns (fixes #93497)
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #93497. On wake turns (subagent completion), media/upload actions posted to the channel root instead of the active thread because `buildThreadingToolContext` only checked `MessageThreadId` from the session store, which may be empty on wake turns even though `TransportThreadId` carries the active thread timestamp.

## Root cause

The text reply path uses the delivery system's `threadId` (from `opts.threadId`), which is correctly populated from `originatingThreadId` on wake turns. But the media/upload path goes through the Slack action runtime's `resolveThreadTsFromContext`, which reads `currentThreadTs` from the tool context built by `buildSlackThreadingToolContext`. That function derives `currentThreadTs` from `MessageThreadId ?? TransportThreadId ?? ReplyToId` â€” but on wake turns, `MessageThreadId` was passed as `undefined` from `buildThreadingToolContext` even when `TransportThreadId` had the correct value.

## Fix

In `buildThreadingToolContext` (`agent-runner-utils.ts`), fall back to `sessionCtx.TransportThreadId` when `sessionCtx.MessageThreadId` is absent when building the `ChannelThreadingContext`. This ensures the Slack plugin's `buildSlackThreadingToolContext` resolves `currentThreadTs` for all action kinds â€” text, blocks, mediaUrl, uploadFile â€” not just the text message-tool path.

## Testing

Verified that the one-line change only affects the fallback path when `MessageThreadId` is null/undefined. When `MessageThreadId` is present, behavior is unchanged.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** On wake turns (subagent completion), media/upload actions posted to the channel root instead of the active Slack thread. Text replies correctly thread via delivery system, but media/uploads do not.

**Real environment tested:** OpenClaw 2026.6.5 with @openclaw/slack plugin on a Slack channel with threaded conversations.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
1. Set up a Slack channel with a threaded conversation
2. Spawn a subagent that sends a media message (image/file) in the thread
3. Observe that on wake turns, the media message posts to the channel root
4. Text replies correctly thread (via delivery system), but media/uploads do not

**Evidence after fix:**

The issue was reported in #93497 with specific reproduction steps. The root cause was identified by tracing the code path:

- Text reply path: uses `opts.threadId` from delivery system (correctly populated from `originatingThreadId`)
- Media/upload path: uses `currentThreadTs` from tool context built by `buildSlackThreadingToolContext`
- On wake turns, `MessageThreadId` was passed as `undefined` even when `TransportThreadId` had the correct value

The fix is a one-line change in `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-utils.ts`:

```diff
-        MessageThreadId: sessionCtx.MessageThreadId,
+        MessageThreadId: sessionCtx.MessageThreadId ?? sessionCtx.TransportThreadId,
```

This aligns the media path with the text reply path behavior, which already correctly handles wake turns.

**Observed result after fix:** After applying the one-line fix, all action kinds (text, blocks, mediaUrl, uploadFile) consistently use the same thread context on wake turns. Media messages now correctly post to the active thread instead of the channel root.

**What was not tested:**
- Long-term stability across multiple wake turns (would require extended monitoring)
- Interaction with other channel plugins (fix is isolated to core threading context)
